english: After the holiday I was broke — the money went like water.

norwegian: Han er ikke akkurat noen lyspære, men han jobber hardt.
english: He's not exactly the sharpest tool in the box, but he works hard.

norwegian: Jeg fikk kalde føtter rett før presentasjonen.
english: I got cold feet right before the presentation.

norwegian: Han gikk rundt grøten i stedet for å si det direkte.
english: He beat around the bush instead of saying it straight.

norwegian: Jeg har svaret på tungespissen, men jeg husker det ikke helt.
english: I have the answer on the tip of my tongue, but I can't quite remember it.

norwegian: Vi snakker alltid rett fra levra med hverandre.
english: We always speak frankly with each other.

norwegian: Jeg er ikke i mitt ess i dag — jeg sov dårlig.
english: I'm not at my best today — I slept badly.

norwegian: Det er jo ikke så vanskelig som du tror.
english: It's not that difficult, as you should already know.

norwegian: Du kommer vel på festen i morgen?
english: You are coming to the party tomorrow, aren't you?

norwegian: Jeg er egentlig ikke så glad i fisk.
english: To be honest, I'm not really that fond of fish.

norwegian: Forresten, har du sett nøklene mine?
english: By the way, have you seen my keys?

norwegian: Han er nemlig veldig flink til å lage mat.
english: The thing is, he's really good at cooking.

norwegian: Altså, jeg skjønner ikke hva du mener.
english: Look, I don't understand what you mean.

norwegian: Det er nok best å ta bussen dit.
english: It's probably best to take the bus there.

norwegian: Det var visst ingen hjemme da jeg ringte.
english: Apparently nobody was home when I called.

norwegian: Kom da, vi rekker bussen hvis vi skynder oss.
english: Come on then, we'll catch the bus if we hurry.

norwegian: Det er liksom ikke det samme uten deg.
english: It's just not the same without you, you know.

norwegian: Jeg visste jo at det ville bli vanskelig.
english: I knew all along that it would be difficult.

norwegian: Hun er vel ikke sint på meg, er hun?
english: She's not angry with me, is she?

norwegian: Vi kan egentlig møtes litt tidligere hvis du vil.
english: We could actually meet a bit earlier if you like.

norwegian: Bussen var nemlig innstilt på grunn av snøen.
english: You see, the bus was cancelled because of the snow.

norwegian: Forresten, vi har ikke bestilt bord ennå.
english: By the way, we haven't booked a table yet.

norwegian: Det skal nok ordne seg, ikke bekymre deg.
english: It'll probably work out fine, don't worry.

norwegian: Altså, det holder ikke å gjøre halvparten av oppgavene.
english: Look, doing half the tasks just isn't good enough.

norwegian: Slapp av da, det er ikke noe å stresse over.
english: Relax then, there's nothing to stress about.

norwegian: Det er jo litt rart at han ikke svarte.
english: It is a bit strange that he didn't reply, isn't it.

norwegian: Vi drar vel ikke dit med tanke på været?
english: We're presumably not going there given the weather?

norwegian: Det var visst ikke første gang det skjedde.
english: Apparently it wasn't the first time that happened.

norwegian: Hvis jeg hadde mer penger, ville jeg reise til Japan.
english: If I had more money, I would travel to Japan.

norwegian: Hvis jeg hadde visst det, ville jeg ikke kommet.
english: If I had known that, I wouldn't have come.

norwegian: Hvis det ikke regnet, ville vi spist ute.
english: If it weren't raining, we would eat outside.

norwegian: Hvis du hadde spurt meg, ville jeg hjulpet deg.
english: If you had asked me, I would have helped you.

norwegian: Om jeg var lege, kunne jeg hjelpe flere mennesker.
english: If I were a doctor, I could help more people.

norwegian: Om jeg bodde i Oslo, ville jeg gå på opera oftere.
english: If I lived in Oslo, I would go to the opera more often.

norwegian: Om jeg snakket bedre norsk, ville jeg søke på den jobben.
english: If I spoke better Norwegian, I would apply for that job.

norwegian: Om det hadde vært varmere, ville vi badet i fjorden.
english: If it had been warmer, we would have swum in the fjord.

norwegian: Tenk om vi bodde i nærheten av havet!
english: Imagine if we lived near the sea!

norwegian: Tenk om vi hadde møttes tidligere!
english: Imagine if we had met earlier!

norwegian: Tenk om vi vant i lotto i morgen!
english: Imagine if we won the lottery tomorrow!

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jeg kunne spille gitar.
english: I wish I could play the guitar.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske sommeren varte lenger.
english: I wish summer lasted longer.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jeg hadde sagt det til ham.
english: I wish I had said it to him.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske vi bodde nærmere familien.
english: I wish we lived closer to the family.

norwegian: Hadde jeg bare studert mer, hadde jeg bestått eksamen.
english: If only I had studied more, I would have passed the exam.

norwegian: Hadde jeg bare tatt bussen, hadde jeg kommet i tide.
english: If only I had taken the bus, I would have arrived on time.

norwegian: Hadde jeg ikke glemt nøklene, ville jeg vært hjemme nå.
english: If I hadn't forgotten the keys, I would be home now.

norwegian: Hadde jeg bare sagt nei, hadde alt vært annerledes.
english: If only I had said no, everything would have been different.

norwegian: Jeg ville reist til Bergen hvis jeg hadde ferie neste uke.
english: I would travel to Bergen if I had holiday next week.

norwegian: Jeg ville ikke jobbet overtid hvis jeg ikke trengte pengene.
english: I wouldn't work overtime if I didn't need the money.

norwegian: Den gamle bilen min starter ikke om vinteren.
english: My old car doesn't start in winter.

norwegian: Det nye huset ligger i en rolig gate.
english: The new house is on a quiet street.

norwegian: De nye skoene er litt for store.
english: The new shoes are a little too big.

norwegian: Det kalde vannet smaker godt etter en lang tur.
english: The cold water tastes good after a long hike.

norwegian: Den nye kollegen min er veldig hyggelig.
english: My new colleague is very friendly.

norwegian: De gamle møblene passer ikke i den nye leiligheten.
english: The old furniture doesn't fit in the new apartment.

norwegian: Den lille kaféen på hjørnet er stengt i dag.
english: The small café on the corner is closed today.

norwegian: Den lange køen gikk veldig sakte.
english: The long queue moved very slowly.

norwegian: De unge studentene bor i sentrum.
english: The young students live in the city centre.

norwegian: Dette interessante møtet varte i tre timer.
english: This interesting meeting lasted for three hours.

norwegian: Det varme brødet luktet fantastisk i butikken.
english: The warm bread smelled fantastic in the shop.

norwegian: De røde bærene i skogen er ikke giftige.
english: The red berries in the forest are not poisonous.

norwegian: Den ødelagte sykkelen sto utenfor inngangen.
english: The broken bicycle was parked outside the entrance.

norwegian: Det store vinduet gir mye lys om morgenen.
english: The large window lets in a lot of light in the morning.

norwegian: Denne korte reisen ga meg mye energi.
english: This short trip gave me a lot of energy.

norwegian: Det grønne gresset ser friskt ut etter regnet.
english: The green grass looks fresh after the rain.

norwegian: Den siste bussen til sentrum gikk for fem minutter siden.
english: The last bus to the city centre left five minutes ago.

norwegian: Det lille rommet ble brukt som kontor.
english: The small room was used as an office.

norwegian: Den mørke skogen er vakker om høsten.
english: The dark forest is beautiful in autumn.

norwegian: De billige flybillettene var utsolgt på én dag.
english: The cheap plane tickets sold out in one day.

norwegian: Den friske luften på fjellet er vanskelig å beskrive.
english: The fresh air on the mountain is difficult to describe.

norwegian: Det er Kari som har bilen.
english: It is Kari who has the car.

norwegian: Det er hun som betaler regningen.
english: It is she who is paying the bill.

norwegian: Det var læreren som forklarte oppgaven.
english: It was the teacher who explained the task.

norwegian: Det er søsteren min som bor i Tromsø.
english: It is my sister who lives in Tromsø.

norwegian: Det er sjefen som bestemmer det.
english: It is the boss who decides that.

norwegian: Det er musikken som gjør ham glad.
english: It is the music that makes him happy.

norwegian: Det er kaffen jeg savner mest.
english: It is the coffee I miss most.

norwegian: Det er norsk jeg vil lære.
english: It is Norwegian I want to learn.

norwegian: Det er den røde jakken jeg liker best.
english: It is the red jacket I like best.

norwegian: Det er den filmen vi snakket om.
english: It is that film we were talking about.

norwegian: Det er deg jeg vil snakke med.
english: It is you I want to speak to.

norwegian: Det var i går jeg så ham.
english: It was yesterday that I saw him.

norwegian: Det var på fredag møtet ble avlyst.
english: It was on Friday that the meeting was cancelled.

norwegian: Det er i sommer vi skal reise til Spania.
english: It is this summer that we are going to Spain.

norwegian: Det er nå vi må handle.
english: It is now that we must act.

norwegian: Det er Oslo jeg kommer fra.
english: It is Oslo I come from.

norwegian: Det er i Bergen det regner mest.
english: It is in Bergen that it rains most.

norwegian: Det er på kjøkkenet vi spiser frokost.
english: It is in the kitchen that we eat breakfast.

norwegian: Det er derfor jeg kom tidlig.
english: That is why I came early.

norwegian: Det var fordi jeg var trøtt at jeg dro hjem.
english: It was because I was tired that I went home.

norwegian: Jeg lurer på hvor toget stopper.
english: I wonder where the train stops.

norwegian: Vet du når butikken stenger?
english: Do you know when the shop closes?

norwegian: Husker du ikke hva hun sa?
english: Don't you remember what she said?

norwegian: Jeg vet ikke om møtet er avlyst.
english: I don't know if the meeting is cancelled.

norwegian: Hun spurte hvem som skulle ta notater.
english: She asked who would take the notes.

norwegian: Fortell meg hvordan du kom deg hit.
english: Tell me how you got here.

norwegian: Vet du hvorfor flyet ble forsinket?
english: Do you know why the plane was delayed?

norwegian: Han spurte om jeg hadde lyst til å bli med.
english: He asked if I wanted to come along.

norwegian: Jeg husker ikke hva vi bestemte oss for.
english: I don't remember what we decided on.

norwegian: Kan du fortelle meg hvor jeg kan finne billettautomaten?
english: Can you tell me where I can find the ticket machine?

norwegian: Hun vet ikke hvem som ringte.
english: She doesn't know who called.

norwegian: Læreren spurte hvorfor vi ikke hadde gjort leksene.
english: The teacher asked why we hadn't done the homework.

norwegian: Vet du hvor lenge de har bodd her?
english: Do you know how long they have lived here?

norwegian: Han spurte hvordan jeg hadde lært norsk.
english: He asked how I had learned Norwegian.

norwegian: Fortell meg hva du synes om forslaget.
english: Tell me what you think about the proposal.

norwegian: Vet du om bussen går om natten?
english: Do you know if the bus runs at night?

norwegian: Hun spurte om vi ville ha kaffe eller te.
english: She asked if we would like coffee or tea.

norwegian: Kan du fortelle meg når kontoret åpner igjen?
english: Can you tell me when the office opens again?

norwegian: Lederen spurte hvem som hadde sendt e-posten.
english: The manager asked who had sent the email.

norwegian: Vi spurte reiselederen om turen var inkludert i prisen.
english: We asked the guide if the trip was included in the price.

norwegian: Læreren fortalte oss ikke hva som kom på prøven.
english: The teacher didn't tell us what was going to be on the test.

norwegian: Butikken åpnes klokka åtte hver morgen.
english: The shop is opened at eight every morning.

norwegian: Billetter selges i skranken.
english: Tickets are sold at the counter.

norwegian: Søknader sendes inn innen fredag.
english: Applications are submitted by Friday.

norwegian: Maten serveres varm fra klokka tolv.
english: The food is served warm from twelve o'clock.

norwegian: Pakkene hentes på postkontoret.
english: Parcels are collected at the post office.

norwegian: Dørene låses automatisk klokka ti.
english: The doors are locked automatically at ten o'clock.

norwegian: Klagene behandles av kundeservice.
english: Complaints are handled by customer service.

norwegian: Lønnen utbetales den femtende hver måned.
english: The salary is paid out on the fifteenth of each month.

norwegian: Avfall sorteres i tre ulike beholdere her.
english: Waste is sorted into three different containers here.

norwegian: Kontrakten signeres av begge parter.
english: The contract is signed by both parties.

norwegian: Huset ble bygget i 1952.
english: The house was built in 1952.

norwegian: Brevet ble sendt av sjefen i går.
english: The letter was sent by the manager yesterday.

norwegian: Maten ble servert kald, og gjestene klaget.
english: The food was served cold, and the guests complained.

norwegian: Vinduet ble knust av en stein.
english: The window was broken by a stone.

norwegian: Passet ble fornyet på politistasjonen forrige uke.
english: The passport was renewed at the police station last week.

norwegian: Han ble ansatt som leder i fjor.
english: He was hired as manager last year.

norwegian: Møtet ble avlyst fordi sjefen var syk.
english: The meeting was cancelled because the manager was ill.

norwegian: Flyet ble omdirigert til Bergen på grunn av tåke.
english: The plane was diverted to Bergen due to fog.

norwegian: Søknaden ble avslått fordi dokumentene manglet.
english: The application was rejected because the documents were missing.

norwegian: Prosjektet ble godkjent av styret på mandag.
english: The project was approved by the board on Monday.

norwegian: Avtalen ble inngått etter lange forhandlinger.
english: The agreement was reached after lengthy negotiations.

norwegian: Jeg vil gjerne bestille et bord til to personer.
english: I would like to book a table for two. (formal/polite — restaurant)

norwegian: Jeg vil ha et bord til to.
english: I want a table for two. (casual — talking to a friend who works there)

norwegian: Kunne du være så snill å sende meg rapporten?
english: Could you please send me the report? (formal — workplace email)

norwegian: Kan du sende meg rapporten?
english: Can you send me the report? (casual — asking a colleague directly)

norwegian: Vennligst fyll ut skjemaet og send det til oss.
english: Please fill in the form and send it to us. (formal — official office letter)

norwegian: Jeg beklager ulempen dette har medført.
english: I apologise for the inconvenience this has caused. (formal — customer service email)

norwegian: Unnskyld, jeg kom litt sent i dag.
english: Sorry, I'm a bit late today. (casual — to a friend)

norwegian: Med vennlig hilsen, Kari Larsen.
english: Yours sincerely, Kari Larsen. (formal — closing a business email)

norwegian: Ses snart! Klem, Kari.
english: See you soon! Hugs, Kari. (casual — closing a message to a friend)

norwegian: Hei, går det bra med deg?
english: Hey, how are you doing? (casual — greeting a friend)

norwegian: Jeg skriver til dere angående søknaden min.
english: I am writing to you regarding my application. (formal — email to an employer)

norwegian: Jeg er svært interessert i stillingen som er lyst ut.
english: I am very interested in the advertised position. (formal — job application)

norwegian: Kan du hjelpe meg litt?
english: Can you help me out? (casual — asking a friend at the café)

norwegian: Kunne du hjelpe meg med dette, takk?
english: Could you help me with this, please? (formal/polite — asking staff at an office)

norwegian: Jeg vil gjerne ha en time hos legen så snart som mulig.
english: I would like an appointment with the doctor as soon as possible. (formal — calling the surgery)

norwegian: Hei! Er du klar for å dra?
english: Hey! Ready to go? (casual — texting a friend)

norwegian: Vi setter pris på tilbakemeldingen din.
english: We appreciate your feedback. (formal — company reply to a customer)

norwegian: Takk for at du sa ifra!
english: Thanks for letting me know! (casual — friend or colleague in chat)

norwegian: Jeg ønsker å søke på stillingen som regnskapsfører.
english: I wish to apply for the position of accountant. (formal — job interview / cover letter)

norwegian: Jeg synes jobben høres ganske kul ut.
english: I think the job sounds pretty cool. (casual — talking to a friend about a job)

norwegian: Hei, hva koster en kopp kaffe her?
english: Hi, how much is a coffee here? (casual — at a café counter)

norwegian: Hun sa at hun alltid spiser frokost.
english: She said that she always eats breakfast.

norwegian: Sjefen sa at rapporten ikke var ferdig.
english: The boss said that the report was not finished.

norwegian: Jeg skjønner at du ikke alltid har tid.
english: I understand that you do not always have time.

norwegian: Kollegaen min sa at han gjerne ville bytte vakt.
english: My colleague said that he would gladly swap shifts.

norwegian: Fordi det regnet, ble vi hjemme.
english: Because it was raining, we stayed home.

norwegian: Vi drar tidligere fordi vi ikke vil stå i kø.
english: We are leaving earlier because we do not want to stand in a queue.

norwegian: Fordi hun ikke visste veien, spurte hun om hjelp.
english: Because she did not know the way, she asked for help.

norwegian: Selv om jeg var trøtt, jobbet jeg videre.
english: Even though I was tired, I kept working.

norwegian: Selv om det var dyrt, kjøpte hun billetten.
english: Even though it was expensive, she bought the ticket.

norwegian: Selv om hun ikke kjente noen der, koste hun seg på festen.
english: Even though she did not know anyone there, she enjoyed herself at the party.

norwegian: Hvis du ikke er sulten, trenger du ikke spise.
english: If you are not hungry, you do not need to eat.

norwegian: Hvis det ikke snør, kan vi sykle dit.
english: If it does not snow, we can cycle there.

norwegian: Når han ikke jobber, liker han å gå tur.
english: When he is not working, he likes to go for a walk.

norwegian: Når du er ferdig, kan vi gå.
english: When you are finished, we can go.

norwegian: Mens hun studerte, lyttet hun alltid til musikk.
english: While she was studying, she always listened to music.

norwegian: Mens barna sov, ryddet foreldrene kjøkkenet.
english: While the children were sleeping, the parents tidied the kitchen.

norwegian: Før hun begynte å jobbe, tok hun en kopp kaffe.
english: Before she started working, she had a cup of coffee.

norwegian: Slik at alle forstår, snakker hun sakte.
english: So that everyone understands, she speaks slowly.

norwegian: Etter at møtet var over, gikk vi på kafé.
english: After the meeting was over, we went to a café.

norwegian: Han spurte om jeg ofte reiser til utlandet.
english: He asked whether I often travel abroad.

norwegian: Da vi kom til hotellet, hadde de allerede gitt rommet til noen andre.
english: When we arrived at the hotel, they had already given the room to someone else.

norwegian: Hun hadde skrevet ferdig rapporten før møtet begynte.
english: She had finished writing the report before the meeting started.

norwegian: Jeg hadde aldri kjørt bil i en storby før jeg begynte å jobbe i Oslo.
english: I had never driven a car in a big city before I started working in Oslo.

norwegian: De hadde allerede ryddet leiligheten da vennene kom.
english: They had already tidied the flat when their friends arrived.

norwegian: Etter at toget hadde kjørt, skjønte vi at vi var på feil stasjon.
english: After the train had left, we realised we were at the wrong station.

norwegian: Jeg visste at kollegaen min hadde søkt på den samme stillingen.
english: I knew that my colleague had applied for the same position.

norwegian: Da vi endelig fant parkeringsplassen, hadde konserten begynt for lenge siden.
english: By the time we finally found the parking space, the concert had started long ago.

norwegian: Hun hadde lært seg litt norsk før hun begynte på kurset.
english: She had taught herself a little Norwegian before she started the course.

norwegian: Vi oppdaget at noen hadde tatt sykkelen vår.
english: We discovered that someone had taken our bike.

norwegian: Læreren hadde allerede delt ut oppgavene da jeg kom inn i klasserommet.
english: The teacher had already handed out the assignments when I came into the classroom.

norwegian: De hadde sett filmen to ganger, men de ville se den igjen.
english: They had seen the film twice, but they wanted to see it again.

norwegian: Vi hadde nettopp kommet hjem da telefonen ringte.
english: We had just arrived home when the phone rang.
